results.text: Copies the hook script to a stable path under the project's .claude/hooks/
results.evidence: eval-1 outputs: .claude/hooks/doc-sweep-revise-push.sh present (-rwxr-xr-x), real bash script

results.text: Writes a config JSON capturing docMode/repoScope AND trigger:"commit" AND an excludeDirs array
Merges a PreToolUse matcher:Bash hook entry into the project settings.json referencing the copied hook + config, preserving any existing settings
results.evidence: settings.json has a PreToolUse/Bash matcher whose command references the hook + config paths

results.text: Offers to seed the review marker on this fresh install (seed-now / review-now / leave) rather than silently leaving it unset
results.evidence: transcript Step 7 offers seed/review/leave and seeds the marker; doc-sweep-revise-marker output contains a HEAD SHA

results.text: Prints a structured summary listing the settings/hook/config paths, the trigger, marker state, bypass tokens, and how to edit/uninstall
results.evidence: transcript Step 8 summary lists settings/hook/config paths, Trigger=commit, marker state, bypass tokens, edit/uninstall line

results.text: Re-running the install does not duplicate the hook entry (idempotent)
results.evidence: Executor stated idempotency would apply but did not actually perform a second run; burden of proof not met

results.text: Removes the guard PreToolUse entry (the one referencing doc-sweep-revise-push.sh) from settings.json
results.evidence: settings-after-uninstall.json no longer contains the Bash/doc-sweep-revise-push.sh PreToolUse entry

results.text: Deletes the copied hook script and the config JSON
results.evidence: deleted-files.txt lists hook script + config JSON deleted; no hook file in eval-2 outputs
settings-after-uninstall.json retains theme:dark and the unrelated PreToolUse/Write matcher

results.text: Leaves the per-clone review marker file in place (does not delete it)
results.evidence: doc-sweep-revise-marker still present after uninstall; deleted-files.txt marks it preserved

results.text: Detects the existing install and offers Reconfigure / Uninstall / Cancel rather than doing a blind fresh install
results.evidence: transcript Step 1 detects doc-sweep-revise-push.sh and offers Reconfigure/Uninstall/Cancel

results.text: On Reconfigure, rewrites the config JSON with the new trigger while preserving the other choices
results.evidence: doc-sweep-revise.json trigger changed to commit; docMode/repoScope/excludeDirs unchanged

results.text: Leaves the review marker file untouched during reconfigure (does not re-seed it)
results.evidence: doc-sweep-revise-marker SHA unchanged from the pre-existing install

results.text: Updates the settings.json matcher only if the hook path or settings location changed
results.evidence: path + settings location unchanged → no matcher update; settings.json command string unmodified

results.text: Scans for likely-vendored directories (submodules, non-root package manifests, known vendor names) and presents candidates for confirmation
results.evidence: transcript Step 4 shows all three signals (.gitmodules, non-root package.json, known names) producing deduped candidates for confirmation

results.text: Persists the confirmed excludeDirs list into .claude/context/audience-rules.md
results.evidence: audience-rules.md output contains the excludeDirs YAML list with the four confirmed dirs

results.text: Mirrors the same excludeDirs into the hook config JSON written next to the hook
results.evidence: doc-sweep-revise.json excludeDirs matches the four entries in audience-rules.md
